title: Review — closed field set and score bound (TRACT-PUBSEAL-03)
description: TRACT-PUBSEAL-03: 'a Review is rejected if it carries any field outside the bounded set §10.4 permits'. §16.5.5 gives Review's CDDL as exactly keys 1-6 with no address/contact production anywhere in it, and its own prose confirms (unlike Offer, whose signed status §16.8 leaves open) that 'a review is the one public object signed by a natural person' — so §16.2's 'signed objects reject unknown keys fail-closed' applies unambiguously. This is the one PUBSEAL-family case this corpus can cover without inventing semantic personal-data classification or resolving an open question — see vectors/README.md for TRACT-PUBSEAL-01/02/04, which are NOT covered and why.

cases.name: valid review -> accepted
cases.note: Keys {1,2,3,4,5,6} ⊆ the known set {1,2,3,4,5,6}; score 4 is within 0..5.

cases.name: extra key 7 (an attempted address smuggle) -> rejected
cases.note: Keys {1..7} is NOT a subset of {1..6}; key 7 is unknown to the Review production. Per §16.2 a signed object rejects this on decode — the smuggled '123 Main St, Springfield' string never has a field to occupy in the first place. (§16.5.5 already notes body/tstr fields remain free text a person could still type an address into — this vector is only about the KEY-level smuggle, not that separate, acknowledged-open gap.)

cases.name: score 7 (out of 0..5) -> rejected
cases.note: §16.5.5: 'score, 0..5' stated directly in the CDDL comment. 7 > 5.
